Project Arts — Ballet UW Model v5 Mgmt Accounts (Dec 2025) Monitoring Data
What's the current projected IRR for Project Arts?
10:24 AM
Based on the latest underwriting model (Ballet UW v5, confirmed current as of April 16, 2026), the projected IRR for Project Arts is 18.4%.
Metric Value
Investment Amount US$25.0M
Current NAV US$31.2M
Projected IRR 18.4%
MOIC 1.6x
Hold Period 3.2 years
WACC 13.5%
The DCF valuation uses an S&P approach with a WACC of 13.5% (unlevered beta 0.169, ERP 4.23%, RFR 1.85%). Revenue is projected to grow from S$12.9M (2026) to S$22.1M (2030), driven primarily by SDM International Pre-school expansion.
Source: Ballet UW v5 → Returns sheet, DCF sheet
10:24 AM
What rollup targets should we consider for the schools portfolio?
10:26 AM
Based on the current portfolio composition and Singapore preschool market dynamics, here are three potential rollup targets ranked by strategic fit: 1. MindChamps PreSchool (SGX-listed)
Currently trading at 8.2x EV/EBITDA vs. your portfolio average of 10.1x. 15 centers across Singapore with strong brand recognition in the premium segment. Would complement SDM International's positioning and add capacity in underserved western districts. 2. Pat's Schoolhouse (private)
8 centers, concentrated in central Singapore. Known for bilingual curriculum alignment with MOE standards. Strong enrollment stability (avg. 92% occupancy). Would provide geographic diversification from your current east-side concentration. 3. Little Skool-House (NTUC subsidiary)
More complex structurally due to NTUC ownership, but 16 centers with government subsidy advantages. Would significantly scale the portfolio and strengthen pricing power with suppliers. Note: These recommendations are based on publicly available market data and current portfolio positioning. Detailed financial analysis would be required before any approach.
Sources: Portfolio entity data, SGX filings, ECDA registry, market research (Q1 2026)
10:27 AM

Recent Research
Market
Singapore Childcare Market Overview — Q1 2026
Total addressable market grew 6.8% YoY to S$3.2B. Government subsidy expansion driving demand in suburban areas. 12 new center licenses approved in Q1.
Generated Apr 1, 2026
Competitor
Competitor Pricing Analysis — Major SG Operators
Average monthly fees increased 4.2% across top-10 operators. Premium segment (>S$2,000/mo) showing strongest growth. SDM International positioned in 75th percentile.
Generated Mar 15, 2026
Exit
Education Sector M&A Activity — Asia 2025
23 transactions closed in APAC education sector. Median EV/EBITDA of 11.3x for childcare platforms. Strategic buyers (Busy Bees, KinderWorld) most active.
Generated Feb 28, 2026
Operational
Enrollment Optimization — Portfolio Benchmark
Portfolio average occupancy at 87% vs. industry benchmark of 91%. Three centers below 80% threshold identified. Infant care slots showing highest demand-supply gap.
Generated Feb 15, 2026
